mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM readings in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index signals supply-side bottlenecks and reduced corporate output capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during inflationary regimes.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 5-day returns in >65% of high-inflation periods, or the mechanism fails to generate a negative Rank IC during CPIAUCSL > 3.0% regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate normalization that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate spread fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >55% of disinflationary periods, or the interaction effect vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payrolls reflects weakening economic activity and lower corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in >60% of observations, or the negative predictive relationship breaks down (Rank IC > -0.01) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDP prints above 2.5% are followed by negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.03) during early-cycle recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment signals a supply-demand mismatch that compresses profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during recessionary transitions.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns in >60% of recessionary transition periods, or the joint signal yields a positive Rank IC during downturns.
